questions.q: I have a face and two hands, but no arms or legs. What am I?
What can travel around the world while staying in a corner?
I speak all languages but have never attended school. What am I?
I have a bed but no pillow, banks but no money, and a mouth but cannot eat. What am I?
What has a thumb and four fingers but is not a hand?
I am full of keys but cannot open any doors. What am I?
What can run but never walks, has a head but never thinks, and has a mouth but never speaks?
I am white when I am dirty and black when I am clean. What am I?
What has a ring but no finger to wear it?
I have cities but no houses, mountains but no trees, and water but no fish. What am I?
What word becomes shorter when you add two letters to it?
I have a face that never frowns and hands that never rest. What am I?
What begins with T, ends with T, and has T in it?
I can be cracked, made, told, and played. What am I?
What gets bigger every time you take away from it?
I am always coming but never arrive. What am I?
What can fill a room but takes up no space?
I am taken out of a quarry and then locked away in a wooden case, yet billions use me daily. What am I?
What has hands but cannot clap?
I am not alive, but I grow. I do not have lungs, but I need air. What am I?
I have a neck but no head. What am I?
I have branches but no leaves, no fruit, and no bark. What am I?
I have an eye but cannot see. What am I?
What question can you never answer 'yes' to?
What kind of room has no windows or doors?
The more there is, the less you see. What am I?
What can be caught but not thrown?
I get smaller every time I take a bath. What am I?
I have teeth but cannot bite. What am I?
I'm light as a feather, yet the strongest person cannot hold me for more than a few minutes. What am I?
The more of this you have, the less you weigh. What is it?
I have hands that move but cannot wave. What am I?
What has a bottom at the top?
What has a crown but is not a king?
I have a face that never frowns but cannot smile either. What am I?
I'm tall when I'm young and short when I'm old. What am I?
What comes down but never goes up?
I have a head and a tail but no body. What am I?
I have branches but no leaves or fruit. What am I?
A classic riddle describes something that 'has an eye but cannot see.' What is traditionally the correct answer?
In riddle form, what has 'thirteen hearts but no other organs'?
A riddle describes something that 'comes at night without being called and leaves during the day without being taken away.' What is it?
I have a spine and pages, but no bones. I can be opened and closed, yet I contain worlds. What am I?
A woman has two sons born at the same hour on the same day of the same year, but they are not twins. How is this possible?
choices: They are from different sets of parents

questions.q: What word reads the same forwards and backwards and is a type of vehicle?

A man lives on the 20th floor of an apartment building. Every morning he takes the elevator down to the ground floor. When he comes home, he takes the elevator to the 10th floor and walks the rest of the way, except on rainy days when he takes the elevator all the way up. Why?
choices: The elevator is broken above the 10th floor
He is afraid of heights on sunny days
He is short and can only reach the 10th-floor button on sunny days
He exercises by walking on dry days

questions.q: I am taken from the ground, refined in a factory, yet I am still the same element I was in nature. What am I?
What 5-letter word becomes shorter when you add letters to the end of it?
I am not a living thing, but I grow; I don't eat, but I'm destroyed by fire; I can be found in deserts and valleys. What am I?
What has a mouth that cannot eat, eyes that cannot see, and a head that cannot think?
The letters in my name spell a number when rearranged. I am a common sport played with a ball and racket. What am I?
I am a five-letter word. Remove my first letter and I become a crime. Remove my first two letters and I become an animal. What word am I?
I am an eight-letter word. My first four letters mean 'a female ruler,' and my last four letters mean 'to strike.' What am I?
I speak all languages but have never studied. I have no mouth, yet travel wherever people go. What am I?
I have keys but produce sound, strings but am not a guitar, and a board but am not a game. What am I?
The man who makes it doesn't want it. The man who buys it doesn't use it. The man who uses it doesn't know it. What is it?
I am taken from nature and processed by heat. I can be colored or remain plain, and I am stretched across frames. What am I?
What has a neck but cannot swallow?
What gets wetter as it dries?
The more of me you take, the more you leave behind. What am I?
I speak without a mouth and hear without ears. I have no body, but come alive with sound. What am I?
What comes once in a lifetime but is experienced by most people?
I am always hungry and must be fed, yet if you give me water, I will die. What am I?
I have branches but no fruit, trunk, or leaves. What am I?
What is seen in the middle of March and April that cannot be seen in any other month?
The more you think about me, the more you think wrongly. What am I?
I have an eye but cannot see. I live in water and am often eaten with potatoes. What am I?
What can you hold in your left hand but not in your right?
What breaks but never falls, and what falls but never breaks?
I am taken out before you use me, put back after you use me. What am I?
What is always in front of you but cannot be seen?
I can open without a key, open without knocking. What am I?
What appears once in every minute, twice in every moment, and yet never in a thousand years?
I am invisible, but I fill every room. When you're alone, I disappear. What am I?
I have all the letters of the alphabet, but only eight letters in my name. What am I?
